page: Microsoft Azure E32s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific data processing tasks. The server achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.62 GB/sec and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 183.59 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 43.89 GB/sec. In contrast, standard compute metrics fall into the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 31862.21 and a PassMark CPU score of 47694.11. Memory bandwidth is measured at 181.75 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 77.85 GB/sec for write operations. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference achieves a strong prompt processing speed of 186.24 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32s_v7 offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads that require large memory footprints without the overhead of high-end GPU accelerators. The absence of local storage means users must rely on external Premium Storage, which introduces a dependency on network-attached storage performance. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, but contains no local storage or GPUs. Architecturally, it supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmark results show strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.62 GB/sec and static web serving at 43.89 GB/sec, alongside average multi-core stress-ng performance. This server is highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, in-memory databases, and caching applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database hosting.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-capacity RAM and advanced Intel matrix extensions for enterprise database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong static web serving throughput
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage or bundled GPUs
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
